item.text:
[demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-hook/demo/rate_limiter.py:13) already has the requested pruning logic:

```python
self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
```

So `allow()` drops timestamps older than the configured window before checking the limit. I didn’t change the file because there was no diff to apply.

--- Learning check ---

Why should the pruning happen before `len(self.calls) < self.limit` instead of after the limit check?
